title: Validate and reflect shaders offline
description: Compile shaders through the validation/SPIR-V toolchain and report errors, interface mismatches, and binding layouts.
body: Use when checking shaders before they reach a driver. Actions: run the offline toolchain (glslang/glslangValidator, naga, or DXC) to compile each stage, run the validation layer where available, and use reflection to dump descriptor bindings, vertex input locations, and uniform offsets. Report compile errors, stage-interface mismatches, and any binding that disagrees with the pipeline layout. Do not rely on a permissive driver accepting code the validator rejects. This skill exposes capability only and must not deploy compiled shaders without runtime permission. Validation signals include a clean validation pass and matching reflected bindings. Risk boundary: validation errors are portability defects; recommend fixing them rather than shipping driver-dependent shaders.
